feedback.text: I find this act an overall net positive for digital consumer rights. However, I do feel somewhat disappointed that video games are not covered. Over the past few years, as digital marketplaces have taken over the games industry, many companies have shut down, revoked and removed games off of store pages and our own games libraries. The most egregious example of this was The Crew published by Ubisoft, in which not only live services were suspended and the game delisted without an end-of-life plan, followed later by the revoking of licenses. This effectively removed the game entirely, with the only way to access it in any way was through illegal means. This horrible precedent has been followed further by ubisoft, but other publishers as well. I feel that the protection of digital game ownership and live service games should be included in this act.
